conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions. Let me start by gathering some basic information:

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children I'm asking about - Alex, who's 18, and Blake, who's 13.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you assess eligibility for both Alex and Blake.

Let me start by asking about Alex, who is 18. Is Alex currently in approved education or training? This would include things like:
- Full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels or equivalent)
- Approved training courses

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. They finished school and aren't studying anywhere right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying about Alex.

Now let me ask about Blake, who is 13. Does Blake live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, it's a bit complicated. Blake does live with me, but their other parent also lives with Blake and already claims Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for explaining that situation. This is indeed an important detail.

Just to clarify: you mentioned that Blake's other parent already claims Child Benefit for Blake. Are you and Blake's other parent living together in the same household, or do you live separately?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: We live separately. Blake lives with their other parent, and that parent is the one claiming Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. So just to make sure I understand correctly: Blake lives with their other parent (not with you), and that parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Is that right?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. Blake lives with their other parent, and they're the one claiming the Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for confirming all of that information. Let me now read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information about your eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. Based on the information you've provided and the official guidance, I can now provide you with a decision on your eligibility for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on my assessment, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for either Alex or Blake. Here's why:

**For Alex (18):**
Alex is not eligible because they are over 16 and not currently in approved education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training (such as A-levels or equivalent).

**For Blake (13):**
Although Blake is under 16, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Blake because:
- Blake lives with their other parent (not with you)
- That parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake
- Only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child

If circumstances were to change in the future - for example, if Alex were to return to full-time education or approved training, or if Blake were to come and live with you and the other parent stopped claiming - your eligibility situation could be different, and you could contact the Child Benefit Office to discuss your options.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in care for 16 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority

Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child in care for 16 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and not in approved education or training. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex has finished school and is not currently in any education or training, they do not meet the age and education criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.

Blake is 13 years old and lives with their other parent, who is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake. According to the rules, only one person can get Child Benefit for a child. Since Blake's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake and Blake lives with that parent (not with you), you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake. The rules state that you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them, or if you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards their upkeep. Since Blake lives with the other parent who is already claiming, you cannot also claim for the same child.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children: Alex (18) and Blake (13). For Alex, eligibility cannot be established because Alex is over 16 and not in approved education or training. For Blake, while Blake is under 16, the child lives with the other parent who is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Under the rules, only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child, so the claimant is not eligible to claim for Blake. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child.

Since Blake lives with the other parent who is already claiming, you cannot also claim for the same child.

For Blake, while Blake is under 16, the child lives with the other parent who is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Under the rules, only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child, so the claimant is not eligible to claim for Blake. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child.
